varieties.name: Tropical
varieties.traits: Small-medium fruit, oval shape, 60-80g
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light tan, slightly gritty, sweet brown-sugar flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Very heavy producer, multiple crops annually

varieties.name: Brown Sugar
varieties.traits: Medium-small fruit, 5-6.25cm long, round to conical
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smooth pinkish-tan, intense brown-sugar sweetness, soft juicy texture
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, May-September harvest

varieties.name: Tikal
varieties.traits: Ovoid shape, fat at one end, 150-310g (can reach 11oz)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm texture, sweet, balanced sweetness and firmness
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropper, December-March main season, May-September minor season

varieties.name: Makok
varieties.traits: Long, pointed fruit, small-medium size (chicken egg size), oval
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smooth brown pulp, sweet aroma, apple pie-like taste with brown sugar and cinnamon notes
varieties.yield_notes: Superior producer, May-November ripening

varieties.name: C55
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit, 60-70g, light brown skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Delicious sweet flavor, slightly gritty texture
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent backyard variety, reliable producer

varieties.name: Alano
varieties.traits: Oval to top-shaped fruit, 3-3.5 inches long, medium-sized
varieties.flesh_fruit: Golden-brown, finely-grained pear texture, brown sugar-honey-cinnamon flavor, very sweet
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, smooth texture, excellent shelf life

varieties.name: Prolific
varieties.traits: Round to conical fruit, smooth skin when ripe (loses fuzzy texture)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Smooth pinkish-tan flesh when ripe
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy bearer, up to 450 pounds per tree per year

varieties.name: Silas Woods
varieties.origin: Homestead, Florida, USA (named after RFCI member)
varieties.traits: Dwarf tree (<20 feet), small round-oval fruit (2-4 inches, 2oz average), spreading habit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Non-gritty, superb eating quality, brown sugar flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely productive, year-round fruiting potential, fruits in 1-2 years

varieties.name: Butterscotch
varieties.origin: Gary Zill selection, Florida, USA (recent release)
varieties.traits: Large football-shaped fruit, 6-7 inches long, 1.2-1.8 lbs, dwarf tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Creamy, richly smooth texture (NO gritty texture), butterscotch flavor, melts in mouth
varieties.yield_notes: Productive, self-pollinating, February-June harvest

varieties.name: Hasya
varieties.traits: Football-shaped, elongated, large fruit (13oz/370g typical)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Reddish hue throughout pulp, excellent eating quality, yellowish-reddish color
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific producer, large upright tree, November-June fruiting

varieties.name: Molix
varieties.traits: Football-shaped, similar to Hasya but darker brown outside, large (13oz/370g), tree has curly leaves
varieties.flesh_fruit: Less red inside than Hasya, exceptionally sweet, fine pear texture, pleasant aroma
varieties.yield_notes: Very tall tree, fine texture, February-May ripening

varieties.name: Morena
varieties.traits: Football-shaped, slightly smaller than Hasya/Molix (12oz/340g), half red/half caramel color
varieties.flesh_fruit: Exceptional flavor, 1-2 seeds (fewer than others)
varieties.yield_notes: Hardy producer, outproduces other Mexican varieties, February-April harvest

varieties.name: Oxkutzcab (Ox)
varieties.traits: Extremely large roundish fruit (up to 28oz/795g), speckled light-colored skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Brown flesh, excellent taste
varieties.yield_notes: Heavily productive tree, May-September cropping

varieties.name: Sapodilla Seedlings
varieties.traits: Very sweet brown-sugar flavor, slightly gritty texture like pear, variable fruit size and quality
varieties.yield_notes: Takes 8-12 years to fruit from seed, many may never fruit due to pollen sterility

varieties.name: Thai Brownie
varieties.traits: Oval fruit, medium size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy producer, seedling will take several years to fruit

varieties.name: BKD-110
varieties.growing_notes: Preferred Singapore variety, documented as already present in Australia since 1983. Suitable for subtropical Queensland.

varieties.name: H.C. Tan
varieties.growing_notes: Popular Singapore variety, documented in Australia since 1983. Subtropical adapted.

varieties.name: Betawi
varieties.traits: Round fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Indonesian variety present in Florida and Australia. Suitable for subtropical conditions.

varieties.name: Ponderosa
varieties.traits: Large-fruited variety, individual fruits up to 350g, poor pollen
varieties.growing_notes: Benefits from cross-pollination and hand-pollination. Popular Philippine variety, available in Queensland.

varieties.name: Russell
varieties.growing_notes: Florida heritage variety, documented as suitable for subtropical Australia.

varieties.name: Martin
varieties.growing_notes: Florida commercial variety, documented presence in Australia since 1983.

varieties.name: Mead
varieties.growing_notes: Florida variety, documented in Australian cultivation since 1983.

varieties.name: Gonzalez
varieties.growing_notes: Florida variety listed for subtropical regions.

varieties.name: Kalipatti 🏆
varieties.origin: Maharashtra, Gujarat, North Karnataka (Indigenous to region)
varieties.traits: Dark green broad thick leaves, oblong oval-shaped fruits, 144.5g average fruit weight
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet pulpy flesh, mellow texture, reddish-brown color, TSS 23.1°Brix, 0.16% acidity
varieties.yield_notes: 350-400 fruits per tree, 156.2kg average yield per mature tree

varieties.name: Cricket Ball 🏆
varieties.traits: Large round fruits, light green leaves, spreading branches
varieties.flesh_fruit: Gritty and granular pulp, sweet, TSS 22.6°Brix, 0.20% acidity
varieties.yield_notes: 157kg per tree, heavy bearer

varieties.name: Pala 🏆
varieties.traits: Small to medium oval or egg-shaped fruits, apex broadly pointed, thin skin, cluster bearing
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, good flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy bearer, fruits borne in clusters

varieties.name: Pilipatti (Pilli Patti) 🏆
varieties.traits: Unique small fruits, oblong elongated shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very soft sweet pulp
varieties.yield_notes: Suitable for high-density planting

varieties.name: Baramasi 🏆
varieties.origin: West Bengal, Bihar, Uttar Pradesh (North India indigenous)
varieties.traits: Medium-sized round fruits, roundish shape
varieties.flesh_fruit: Medium-sized with good quality
varieties.yield_notes: 12 months yield (Baramasi means 'all year'), continuous bearing

varieties.name: Kirthibarti (Kirti Bharti) 🏆
varieties.traits: Medium-sized oval fruits, 4-6 ridges on rind, rough medium-thick buff-colored skin, rounded apex
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet pulp
varieties.yield_notes: Good for long distance transport

varieties.name: CO-1
varieties.origin: TNAU Coimbatore (1972) - Cricket Ball × Oval hybrid
varieties.traits: Long oval (egg-shaped), medium size, 125g mean fruit weight
varieties.flesh_fruit: Granular texture, reddish-brown color, very sweet, TSS 18°Brix
varieties.yield_notes: 175-200kg per tree, bearing after 4 years, superior to both parents

varieties.name: CO-2
varieties.origin: TNAU Coimbatore (1974) - Clonal selection from Baramasi
varieties.traits: Medium height tree, oblong-round fruit, medium-sized, 125-150g
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft juicy flesh, sweet, gritty, light brown color with pleasant aroma, TSS 23°Brix
varieties.yield_notes: Good bearer

varieties.name: CO-3
varieties.origin: TNAU (released variety) - Cricket Ball × Vavivalasa hybrid
varieties.traits: Light brown oblong-shaped fruit, suitable for high-density planting
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet taste, TSS 24.2°Brix
varieties.yield_notes: 157kg annual yield per tree, bearing from 4th year onwards

varieties.name: PKM-1
varieties.origin: TNAU Periyakulam (1981) - Clonal selection from Guthi
varieties.traits: Dwarf stature tree, oblong fruit, thin skin, 80g average weight
varieties.flesh_fruit: Buttery and very sweet pulp, rich in sugars, TSS 23°Brix
varieties.yield_notes: Medium-sized fruits

varieties.name: PKM-2
varieties.traits: Oblong shape
varieties.growing_notes: Promising hybrid for South Indian conditions, suitable for Kerala.

varieties.name: PKM-3
varieties.origin: TNAU Periyakulam (1994) - Guthi × Cricket Ball hybrid
varieties.traits: Vertical growth habit (ideal for high density), big-sized oval fruits, cluster-bearing habit
varieties.yield_notes: 14 tonnes per hectare

varieties.name: PKM-4
varieties.origin: TNAU Periyakulam (2003) - Clonal selection from PKM-1
varieties.traits: Spindle-shaped fruits, suitable for dry flakes production
varieties.flesh_fruit: Attractive light pinkish honey-brown color, crisp and sweet flesh, TSS 24°Brix

varieties.name: PKM-5
varieties.origin: TNAU Periyakulam (2007) - Clonal selection from Virudunagar
varieties.growing_notes: Recent TNAU release adapted to South Indian conditions, suitable for Kerala cultivation.

varieties.name: DHS-1
varieties.origin: UAS Dharwad, Karnataka - Kalipatti × Cricket Ball hybrid
varieties.traits: Vigorous tree, round to slightly oblong fruits, 150g mean weight
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet, soft granular mellowing flesh, light orange color, TSS 26°Brix
varieties.yield_notes: High yield

varieties.name: DHS-2
varieties.origin: UAS Dharwad, Karnataka - Kalipatti × Cricket Ball hybrid
varieties.traits: Round fruit, 5.63cm breadth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light orange, TSS 23°Brix

varieties.name: DHS-1-2/1
varieties.origin: UHS Bagalkot (2014) - Clonal selection from DHS-1
varieties.growing_notes: Recent improved selection from DHS-1, suitable for Kerala's tropical conditions.

varieties.name: Guthi 🏆
varieties.traits: Small-sized oval fruit, apex broadly pointed, cluster bearing
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet pulp
varieties.growing_notes: Parent of PKM-1, PKM-2, PKM-3. Resistant to leaf spot disease.

varieties.name: Oval 🏆
varieties.traits: Oval-shaped fruits (hence name)
varieties.growing_notes: Parent of CO-1 hybrid. Moderately tolerant to leaf spot.

varieties.name: Dwarapudi (Dwarpudi) 🏆
varieties.traits: Fruits resemble Cricket Ball but smaller in size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet pulp, great demand, very large crisp fruit
varieties.growing_notes: Tolerant to leaf spot disease.

varieties.name: Calcutta Round (Calcutta Special Round) 🏆
varieties.traits: Round fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Most susceptible to leaf spot disease among major varieties.

varieties.name: Chhatri 🏆
varieties.traits: Lower quality compared to Kalipatti
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding variety

varieties.name: Dhola Diwani 🏆
varieties.traits: Egg-shaped fruits
varieties.yield_notes: Good quality yield

varieties.name: Vavivalasa (Vavi Valsa) 🏆
varieties.growing_notes: Parent of CO-3 hybrid. Traditional variety.

varieties.name: Murabba (Murrabba) 🏆
varieties.growing_notes: Popular in Maharashtra, adaptable to various Indian regions.

varieties.name: Bangalore 🏆
varieties.traits: Round fruits, smooth texture
varieties.flesh_fruit: Excellent taste
varieties.growing_notes: Well-suited for wide range of climates.

varieties.name: Jonnavalasa Round 🏆
varieties.traits: Small to medium size, round shape

varieties.name: Tagarampudi 🏆
varieties.growing_notes: Highly susceptible to leaf spot disease - requires intensive management.

varieties.name: Gavarayya 🏆
varieties.growing_notes: Highly susceptible to leaf spot disease.

varieties.name: Virudhnagar (Virudunagar) 🏆
varieties.growing_notes: Parent of PKM-5, moderately tolerant to leaf spot.

varieties.name: Pot Sapota
varieties.traits: Small fruit, egg-shaped with sharp top, fruiting starts in pot
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet and aromatic

varieties.name: Barada
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional Indian variety suitable for tropical cultivation.

varieties.name: Pata
varieties.growing_notes: Listed among Indian cultivated varieties suitable for tropical regions.

varieties.name: Sawo Manila
varieties.traits: Oval fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Popular in Philippines and Indonesia. Already present in Australia.

varieties.name: Pineras
varieties.yield_notes: High-yielding variety

varieties.name: Kra Suay
varieties.traits: Small-fruited, elongated

varieties.name: Kai Hahn
varieties.traits: Large-fruited
varieties.growing_notes: Present in Australia since 1983.

varieties.name: Jantung (C62)
varieties.traits: Better early establishment growth, larger plant size and stem girth, broad conical canopy
varieties.yield_notes: 21.1% fruit set, 33.2 tonnes/hectare, less prolific flowering but high fruit set

varieties.name: Subang (C63)
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended Malaysian clone for cultivation.

varieties.name: Betawi (C61)
varieties.traits: Round fruits

varieties.name: Sawo Kulon
varieties.traits: Round fruits

varieties.name: Sawokecik (Mini Sapodilla)
varieties.traits: Mini-sized fruits
varieties.growing_notes: Grown mainly for durable wood (rosewood for woodcarving).

varieties.name: C4, C54, C55, C60
varieties.growing_notes: Malaysian Department of Agriculture registered clones suitable for tropical cultivation. All adapted to monsoon climate similar to Kerala.
